scenarios.e2e: No process e2e until audit sessions are wired through provider-daemon.
scenarios.next_test: Add audit-budget demand/spend/backlog tests proving audit demand is capped and backlog is explicit.

scenarios.e2e: Manual or nightly multi-provider outage, not PR-blocking CI.
scenarios.next_test: Keep as simulator calibration until placement-diversity params exist, then add keeper candidate-selection tests.

scenarios.e2e: Provider returns corrupt bytes or invalid proof and user-gateway rejects the response.
scenarios.next_test: Add invalid-proof or wrong-data keeper tests proving no corrupt payment, repair start, and slash/jail simulation gates.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; this is a parameter-calibration fixture.
scenarios.next_test: Compare elasticity slope, reference price, price-step timing, and demand recovery before encoding governance defaults.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e until MsgSubmitEvidence and protocol retrieval sessions exist.
scenarios.next_test: Add evidence-bond escrow, burn-on-expiry, conviction gating, bounty payout, spam-throttle, and deputy-reputation tests.

scenarios.e2e: Burst traffic e2e after overlay semantics are implemented.
scenarios.next_test: Add spend-window tests for saturation signaling, fail-closed expansion, TTL, and cap-bound rejection.

scenarios.e2e: Drive hot retrieval pressure, trigger funded overflow capacity, assert overlay routes serve reads after readiness and expire without changing base slot durability.
scenarios.next_test: Add tests proving saturation signals can buy bounded temporary overlay routes, providers prove readiness before routing, routes expire by TTL, and spend caps fail closed.

scenarios.e2e: Create, commit, wait through duration, then fetch after expiry and assert an expired-content response with no owner retrieval debit.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper/gateway tests proving post-expiry reads return explicit expired-content responses, do not count as live unavailable reads, and do not debit retrieval escrow.

scenarios.e2e: Optional provider restart e2e; keeper coverage should be the first artifact.
scenarios.next_test: Add missed-epoch window tests proving intermittent failures create health evidence without triggering repair churn.

scenarios.e2e: Hot retrieval burst against heterogeneous providers after gateway/provider telemetry exists; assert promoted providers receive hot traffic and can later demote on regression.
scenarios.next_test: Add capability-tier keeper/runtime tests proving measured providers can become high-bandwidth eligible and hot retrieval routing prefers them without over-capacity assignment.
scenarios.target: provider capability and hot-route policy tests

scenarios.e2e: Hot retrieval burst that intentionally saturates promoted providers; assert demotion events fire and retrievals continue through fallback capacity.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper/runtime tests proving promoted providers demote after sustained saturation and hot routing falls back without data-loss or over-capacity assignment.
scenarios.target: capability demotion and hot-route failover tests

scenarios.e2e: Gateway happy-path smoke remains sufficient; do not add a slow failure e2e for the control case.
scenarios.next_test: Add no-op epoch tests proving healthy providers do not accrue evidence, repair, reward exclusion, jail, or slash state.

scenarios.e2e: Provider submits an invalid liveness proof while retrieval bytes remain clean; assert proof rejection and repair.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper tests proving invalid liveness proofs create hard evidence, trigger repair, and apply slash/jail gates without requiring corrupt retrieval bytes.

scenarios.e2e: Do not mirror this as process e2e; keep it as simulator/CI artifact work.
scenarios.next_test: Use sweep reports to tune repair throughput, placement headroom, retrieval pricing, and provider P&L before keeper defaults.

scenarios.e2e: Slow-path only after keeper reward accounting is stable.
scenarios.next_test: Add quota shortfall and synthetic-fill tests proving lazy responsibility is excluded from base rewards without soft-fault slashing.

scenarios.e2e: Provider set with one dominant operator; assert hot and normal deal placement stay within operator caps and replacement falls back only with explicit evidence.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper/runtime tests proving deterministic placement and repair candidate selection respect per-deal operator caps while surfacing fallback reasons.
scenarios.target: operator identity and assignment cap keeper tests

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; this is a parameter-calibration fixture.
scenarios.next_test: Compare quote affordability bounds, price-step timing, and demand rejection semantics before encoding governance defaults.

scenarios.e2e: Hot-service retrieval burst after telemetry exists; assert tier counts and provider payouts reflect latency without breaking read availability.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper/runtime tests proving retrieval telemetry maps to Platinum/Gold/Silver/Fail tiers, tiered rewards are deterministic, and Fail-tier QoS does not become slashable hard evidence.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e; validate with keeper tests and simulator sweeps first.
scenarios.next_test: Add epoch pricing tests for floors, ceilings, utilization target, retrieval-demand target, and max step bps.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; validate collateral policy with simulator scenarios before provider bond keeper tests.
scenarios.next_test: Compare minimum bond, per-slot collateral, slash sizing, and underbonded repair urgency before keeper bond state or provider top-up UX is implemented.
scenarios.target: provider bond and assignment collateral calibration

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; this is a parameter-calibration fixture.
scenarios.next_test: Compare provider cost assumptions, price floors, reward buffers, and whether cost telemetry should trigger governance review before encoding market defaults.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; validate churn policy with simulator sweeps and then add keeper drain/replacement tests.
scenarios.next_test: Compare churn caps, minimum active-provider floor, draining notice, replacement throughput, and price-floor response before keeper drain semantics are implemented.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; validate supply recovery with simulator scenarios before provider registration or promotion keeper tests.
scenarios.next_test: Compare reserve sizing, entry caps, probation windows, utilization triggers, and readiness semantics before keeper provider lifecycle state is implemented.
scenarios.target: provider lifecycle and supply recovery calibration

scenarios.e2e: Small devnet with no spare provider capacity after keeper behavior is stable.
scenarios.next_test: Add tests proving no eligible replacement emits backoff, preserves capacity constraints, and does not over-assign providers.
scenarios.target: candidate selection and repair backoff keeper tests

scenarios.e2e: Provider replacement with a pending SP that never catches up; assert timeout/retry state before process-level promotion.
scenarios.next_test: Add tests proving pending replacements must submit readiness before promotion, time out when they fail catch-up, and respect retry cooldown and attempt caps.
scenarios.target: pending-provider readiness and retry keeper tests

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; validate with keeper pricing tests and simulator sweeps first.
scenarios.next_test: Compare retrieval demand targets, price-step clamps, smoothing windows, and burst response before encoding retrieval pricing defaults.

scenarios.e2e: Create deal with one failing provider upload and verify replacement before first content commit.
scenarios.next_test: Add setup-phase replacement tests proving failed initial upload selects a system provider and does not imply fraud.

scenarios.e2e: Kill one provider-daemon during retrieval and assert reads stay available while repair starts.
scenarios.next_test: Add a keeper test where a slot crosses missed-epoch threshold, enters repair, selects a deterministic pending provider, and later promotes.

scenarios.e2e: Client repeatedly stages uploads without commit; assert provider-daemon cleanup bounds disk pressure and committed reads remain available.
scenarios.next_test: Add provider-daemon and user-gateway tests proving abandoned provisional generations are capped, cleaned after TTL, and surfaced through dry-run/apply cleanup UX without affecting committed deal state.
scenarios.target: provider-daemon staged cleanup and gateway preflight

scenarios.e2e: Create, commit, close early, and assert unearned storage escrow is refunded while earned fees remain paid to eligible providers.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper and gateway tests proving storage quote parity, upfront lock-in, earned-fee payout, early close refund, expiry auto-close, and zero hidden outstanding escrow.

scenarios.e2e: Create, commit, wait through duration, assert the deal expires with no outstanding escrow and no active slots.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper and gateway tests proving fully earned deals auto-expire, stop serving active responsibility, leave no hidden escrow, and expose final query state.

scenarios.e2e: Commit content, force one provider through quota delinquency, assert earned fees for non-compliant responsibility are burned while compliant providers are paid and reads remain available.
scenarios.next_test: Add keeper and gateway tests proving delinquent storage responsibility loses earned-fee payout and records a burn without confusing storage lock-in, repair, or availability accounting.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e until keeper reward gating is complete.
scenarios.next_test: Add tests proving idle or non-compliant responsibility cannot farm base rewards profitably.

scenarios.e2e: Provider timeout/blackhole e2e after keeper state is deterministic.
scenarios.next_test: Add per-slot delinquency tests for repeated non-response, reward exclusion, repair start, and replacement selection.

scenarios.e2e: No process e2e yet; this is a parameter-calibration fixture.
scenarios.next_test: Compare storage floors, base rewards, and provider cost assumptions before encoding governance defaults.

scenarios.e2e: Public retrieval spike against one deal with requester/sponsor funding.
scenarios.next_test: Add sponsored-session tests proving public demand pays providers without draining owner escrow unexpectedly.

scenarios.e2e: Synthetic wash traffic e2e only after keeper accounting exists.
scenarios.next_test: Add retrieval fee, burn, credit-cap, and requester-paid session accounting tests.
scenarios.target: session fee and credit-cap keeper tests

scenarios.e2e: Provider refuses retrieval responses; gateway routes around and records attributable failure.
scenarios.next_test: Add tests for threshold non-response evidence and deputy-served miss accounting before punitive policy.
